turns.thought: [OBSERVE] Iteration 932: gbest is 19.899, unchanged for 200 iterations. Diversity is extremely low (normalized ~2.3e-11). All particles have near-zero velocity (velocity_zero_ratio=1.0, velocity_norm_mean ~7.9e-10). Fitness values are virtually identical (std ~7e-15), indicating all particles occupy the same point in search space. No improvement, extreme convergence. | [DIAGNOSE] The swarm has completely collapsed to a single local optimum. The Rastrigin function's strong multimodality has trapped the swarm in a deep basin far from the global minimum (0). The root cause is premature convergence due to loss of diversity; all exploratory pressure has vanished. The swarm is stalled without any mechanism to escape. | [STRATEGIZE] The goal is to introduce strong randomness to break out of the local basin and restore diversity. A large fraction of particles must be reinitialized to fresh random positions to explore other regions. Given the extreme convergence, reset_worst_particles with a high ratio (0.5) and velocity reset is more effective than local perturbations (mutation) or jumps around the current best (levy_flight), because the entire swarm is essentially identical. A higher inertia weight after reset will sustain exploration. This strategy directly combats the collapse by replenishing the gene pool.
